description_long: This object consists of three thick, white pillar candles fused together at their bases, with a realistic, uneven pool of melted wax spreading outward. The candles vary in height, each with a visible wick at the top, and the surface is marked by naturalistic wax drips and folds. A prominent, delicate spider web is draped across the front and side, adding a decorative, slightly eerie touch. The wax has a semi-glossy finish, giving it a soft sheen under light. The base is irregular and organic, mimicking the way melted wax would spread on a flat surface. The overall appearance suggests a decorative, possibly seasonal or themed candle cluster suitable for urban environments.
description: A cluster of three white pillar candles with melted wax pooling at the base and a spider web draped across the front, featuring visible wicks and naturalistic wax drips.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three candles, the spider web detail, and the most prominent wax drips and pooling.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tallest candle and the rear of the wax pool, with the spider web partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the two main candles and the smooth, melted wax base, with minimal web detail.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the tops of all three candles, the wicks, and the spider web extending to the side.
